creator: koobs@FreeBSD.org
text:
@Larry Anyone may commit to any repository with a review accepted by anyone from that repository.

There is a #docs 'project' [1] in Phabricator anyone may add as a reviewer to a differential, which members in that project will then see.

As far as the change proposal here goes, this sounds like:

1) Fix the -fno-common issue (was -fcommon tried as a temporary workaround?)
2) Identify a suitable replacement, if any
3) Remove the reference

Notably the debian cpu debian package [2] at least remains maintained (at debian) and building with later versions of GCC that also now default to -fno-common.

Might be worth browsing the patches and previous bug reports there.

[1] https://reviews.freebsd.org/project/members/72/
[2] https://sources.debian.org/patches/cpu/1.4.3-13/
